errors.description: PlatformUser id was not resolved (does not exist, or soft-deleted past the retention hold + hard-deleted).

The referenced Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted. Platform staff MUST be MFA-enrolled before receiving platform_admin credentials. Fix: instruct the target Identity to enrol MFA via the identity-service self-service flow, then retry the hire.
The referenced Identity has NULL email_verified_at. Platform staff MUST have verified email before hire.

Another platform_users row already references the same identity_id (UNIQUE constraint violation). An Identity can back at most ONE PlatformUser. If this Identity is a re-hire, un-purge or manually restore the prior PlatformUser row rather than creating a new one.

State-machine violation. Valid transitions: pending→active, active→suspended, suspended→active, {pending|active|suspended}→offboarded. Any other attempt (e.g. offboarded→active) refuses with this error + names the current status + attempted target status in the error detail.
POST /suspend was called without a reason, or with a reason under 20 chars. Suspensions require documented justification.
POST /offboard was called without a reason, or with a reason outside 20–1000 chars.

Attempted to offboard a PlatformUser who is a manager (manager_id on other active/suspended rows points at them). Reassign the reports via `platform-user:reassign-manager` first, then re-attempt the offboard.
The proposed manager_id points at a soft-deleted or offboarded PlatformUser. Choose an active or suspended manager.
A PlatformUser cannot manage themselves (unless the `allow_self` bootstrap flag is passed to `manager_belongs_to_platform`, reserved for founder-only edge cases).
The proposed manager chain would create a cycle (A→B→C→A). Reject at write time to protect the org-chart accessor.
Request refused by the `platform.mfa_required` middleware. The PlatformUser's Identity has since disabled MFA. Re-enrol MFA via identity-service, then re-authenticate.

PurgeOffboardedPlatformUsersJob refused to hard-delete because RevokePlatformUserAccessJob has not completed successfully. Ops investigation required. Retryable after ops fixes the revocation failure.

Attempted to hard-delete a PlatformUser row still inside the 90-day retention hold. Wait for the hold to expire, or if compliance signs off on early purge, use the manual `platform-user:early-purge` command (super_admin + compliance co-sign required).
OnCallResolver timed out (both PagerDuty AND internal fallback failed to respond in time). Retryable. Escalation tooling should fall back to Slack broadcast to the department channel.
A mutating command was invoked without --reason=<text> when `platform-user.commands.require_reason` is on (staging + prod default).
The proposed manager assignment would push the org-chart chain past `platform-user.org_chart.max_depth` (default 8). Flatten the chain before retrying.
